description: Rules to ensure the application remains fast, responsive, and lightweight by default.
philosophy: Performance is a feature, not an optimization. Every component must be built with performance in mind from the start.

rules.name: Component Memoization Mandate
rules.description: All non-trivial components that receive props MUST be wrapped in `React.memo` to prevent unnecessary re-renders.
guidelines: If a component's output is the same for the same props, it must be memoized.
Use `useCallback` for all functions passed as props to memoized children to maintain referential equality.
Use `useMemo` for computationally expensive calculations or objects/arrays created during render.
rules.consequence: Failure to memoize components leads to cascading re-renders that degrade UI performance.

rules.name: Lazy Loading Mandate
rules.description: Large components, pages, and heavy third-party libraries MUST be lazy-loaded.

implementation.componentsAndPages: Use `React.lazy()` with `<Suspense>` to code-split routes and large, off-screen components.
implementation.libraries: Use dynamic `import()` for heavy libraries like charting or data processing libraries, loading them only when needed.

rules.purpose: To reduce the initial bundle size and improve the application's First Contentful Paint (FCP) and Time to Interactive (TTI).

rules.name: Image Optimization
rules.description: All images MUST be optimized for the web and served in modern formats.
guidelines: Use a component like Next.js `<Image>` or a similar library that provides automatic optimization, resizing, and format conversion (e.g., to WebP/AVIF).
Specify `width` and `height` attributes to prevent layout shifts.
Use the `loading="lazy"` attribute for all images that are below the fold.

rules.name: Bundle Size Auditing
rules.description: The application's bundle size MUST be monitored to prevent bloat.
rules.implementation: Integrate a bundle analyzer tool (like `vite-bundle-visualizer`) into the build process to regularly audit what is contributing to the final bundle size.
rules.action: Any Pull Request that significantly increases the bundle size requires justification and review.
